2603.20170.title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs for Theory-of-mind Reasoning
2603.20170.paper_summary: Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning with Large Language Models (LLMs) requires inferring how people's implicit, evolving beliefs shape what they seek and how they act under uncertainty -- especially in high-stakes settings such as disaster response, emergency medicine, and human-in-the-loop autonomy. Prior approaches either prompt LLMs directly or use latent-state models that treat beliefs as static and independent, often producing incoherent mental models over time and weak reasoning in dynamic contexts. We introduce a structured cognitive trajectory model for LLM-based ToM that represents mental state as a dynamic belief graph, jointly inferring latent beliefs, learning their time-varying dependencies, and linking belief evolution to information seeking and decisions. Our model contributes (i) a novel projection from textualized probabilistic statements to consistent probabilistic graphical model updates, (ii) an energy-based factor graph representation of belief interdependencies, and (iii) an ELBO-based objective that captures belief accumulation and delayed decisions. Across multiple real-world disaster evacuation datasets, our model significantly improves action prediction and recovers interpretable belief trajectories consistent with human reasoning, providing a principled module for augmenting LLMs with ToM in high-uncertainty environment. https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ICML_submission-6373/
2603.20170.paper_summary_zh: 心智理論（ToM）推理與大型語言模型（LLMs）需要推斷人們的隱性、演變中的信念如何塑造他們在不確定情況下的尋求和行動——特別是在高風險環境中，如災難應對、緊急醫學和人機協作自主性。先前的方法要麼直接提示LLMs，要麼使用潛在狀態模型，將信念視為靜態和獨立的，這常常導致隨時間產生不連貫的心理模型和在動態情境中的弱推理。我們引入了一個結構化的認知軌跡模型，用於基於LLM的ToM，該模型將心理狀態表示為動態信念圖，聯合推斷潛在信念，學習其隨時間變化的依賴性，並將信念演變與信息尋求和決策聯繫起來。我們的模型貢獻了（i）一種從文本化的概率陳述到一致的概率圖模型更新的新投影，（ii）一種基於能量的信念相互依賴的因子圖表示，以及（iii）一種基於ELBO的目標，捕捉信念累積和延遲決策。在多個現實世界的災難撤離數據集中，我們的模型顯著改善了行動預測，並恢復了與人類推理一致的可解釋信念軌跡，為在高不確定性環境中增強LLMs與ToM提供了一個原則性模塊。 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ICML_submission-6373/
2603.20170.author: Ruxiao Chen et.al.
2603.20170.authors: Ruxiao Chen, Xilei Zhao, Thomas J. Cova, Frank A. Drews, Susu Xu
